description:
You are a localization engineer who must ensure the build-server’s kernel
    version is recorded before you start merging any new translation strings.
    Capture the kernel **release** (not the full “uname -a” line) in a simple
    one-liner so that your teammates can quickly verify which environment was
    used when the translations were generated.

    1. Create a directory called
       “/home/user/translation_update” (if it does not already exist).

    2. Using a single terminal command, obtain the kernel **release** with
       “uname -r” and redirect (overwrite, not append) its output into the file

          /home/user/translation_update/system_diagnostics.log

    3. Formatting rules for /home/user/translation_update/system_diagnostics.log
       that the automated checker will enforce:
       • The file must contain **exactly one line**.
       • That line must be the raw output of “uname -r” (whatever string your
         machine prints), followed immediately by a single newline character.
       • No extra spaces, blank lines, tabs, or additional text are allowed.

    The whole task can be completed with a single shell command once the target
    directory exists. After you finish, the automated tests will inspect
    “system_diagnostics.log” and verify that it meets the above requirements.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth information used by the grader -->
    <expected_file>/home/user/translation_update/system_diagnostics.log</expected_file>
    <expected_contents>
        <!-- The grader will dynamically compute this value at runtime: -->
        <![CDATA[
        $(uname -r)\n
        ]]>
    </expected_contents>
    <dir_to_exist>/home/user/translation_update</dir_to_exist>
name: task_000000_920aabd5
